pick.rationale: Arizona is a heavy favorite over Villanova based on elite advanced metrics, ranking 3rd nationally in both KenPom and Torvik with top-10 offensive efficiency and the nation's 3rd-best defense. Villanova, while solid at 33rd–36th overall, has significantly weaker offensive and defensive efficiency metrics compared to the Wildcats. Although Arizona previously dealt with an injury to star Koa Peat, no recent reports confirm his absence for this game, and even without him, the talent and statistical gap between these teams is substantial. Arizona’s dominant 31–2 record against a brutal Big 12 schedule further underscores their superiority over Villanova’s 24–8 Big East campaign.

reasoningStep.title: Arizona vs Villanova Round of 32 Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: Arizona holds a massive statistical and talent advantage over Villanova, making them a high-confidence pick despite minor prior injury concerns.
evidence: Arizona ranks 3rd in both KenPom and Torvik; Villanova ranks 33rd–36th.
Arizona's offense (7th–10th) and defense (3rd) are elite; Villanova's are merely solid (42nd–44th offense, 37th defense).
Arizona went 31–2 in the Big 12; Villanova was 24–8 in the Big East.
No confirmed reports indicate Koa Peat or other key Arizona players are unavailable for this game.

toolCalls.summary: Returned combined Torvik/KenPom ratings for 2 teams.

modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.
